title: Macramé wall pocket organiser
subtitle: A knotted wall-mounted organiser with two pockets for letters, notebooks, or small plants
excerpt: Knot a wall-mounted pocket organiser with two open pockets from 5 mm natural cotton macramé cord. The pockets form from a panel of square knots folded and sewn along the base and sides. The finished piece is about 30 cm wide and 50 cm tall and mounts from a wooden dowel.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional macramé knotting practice. Wall-mounted cord organisers with pockets are documented in British and Scandinavian craft teaching.

glossaryTerms.definition: A flat, symmetrical knot made with two working cords around two filler cords. The foundation knot structure for both the backing panel and the pocket panels.
Two consecutive half hitches by the same working cord around the same filler cord. Used to make the horizontal ridge rows that define the pocket tops in this organiser.
A row of double half hitches in which every working cord hitches onto a single horizontal holding cord, creating a firm ridge line.

content.text: ridge rows to mark the pocket tops. The
rows are the technical step that distinguishes an intermediate from a beginner project; they require even, consistent tension on each hitch.

items.name: Strong thread and needle for pocket seams

content.text: Step 1: Mount cords on the dowel'
Cut 20 cords each 3 metres long. Mount each cord on the dowel using a lark's head knot. You will have 40 working cord ends. The dowel forms the top hanging bar.
Work alternating square knots from the dowel downward for 45 cm. This is the backing panel. Keep the knots evenly spaced at 2 cm intervals.

The two pockets are knotted as separate flat panels, each about 28 cm wide and 20 cm deep, using the same alternating square knot pattern. Knot the pocket panels before attaching them to the backing panel. Use the same cord weight for consistency.

Position the first pocket panel on the lower third of the backing panel and the second on the upper third. Sew or knot the bottom and side edges of each pocket to the backing panel using a strong thread or by knotting the cord tails of the pocket to the backing panel cords. Trim and finish the backing panel cords at the base with a fringe or overhand knots.

items.problem: The pockets are too shallow to hold anything
items.cause: The pocket panels were knotted to fewer than 20 cm depth
items.fix: Knot each pocket panel to at least 22 cm deep before attaching. The square knot structure packs somewhat when the pocket is in use; extra depth is worth the added knotting time.

items.problem: The horizontal ridge rows at the pocket tops are uneven with some hitches tighter than others
items.cause: Inconsistent tension on individual double half hitches
items.fix: Hold the horizontal holding cord taut and at a consistent angle as each working cord hitches onto it. Lay the holding cord across the T-pins at the correct angle before beginning the row.
